questions.question_text: Under RSA 356-B, how many units trigger the Public Offering Statement registration requirement when marketed to the public?
questions.explanation: RSA 356-B:49 exempts a condominium from registration and Public Offering Statement requirements if not more than 10 units are included in the project (unless time-sharing interests are involved). The threshold for triggering full registration is therefore more than 10 units. A 'small condominium' of 25 or fewer units may still qualify for abbreviated registration under RSA 356-B:51.

questions.question_text: A developer plans a 3-unit condominium project. What registration obligation applies under RSA 356-B?
questions.correct_answer: The project is exempt from registration and POS requirements
distractors: A full POS must be filed with the AG
The developer must obtain a DES permit
questions.explanation: RSA 356-B:49 exempts condominium offerings of not more than 10 units from the chapter's registration and Public Offering Statement requirements (unless time-sharing interests are involved or the structure is being used to evade the law). A 3-unit project falls well within that exemption, although condominium instruments still must be recorded with the Registry of Deeds.

questions.question_text: Which state office reviews condominium Public Offering Statements in New Hampshire?
questions.explanation: The Attorney General's Consumer Protection and Antitrust Bureau administers RSA 356-B and reviews Public Offering Statements for condominium projects that exceed the 10-unit RSA 356-B:49 exemption.

questions.question_text: Where must condominium instruments be recorded in New Hampshire?
questions.explanation: Condominium instruments (declaration, bylaws, site plans, and floor plans) must be recorded with the county Registry of Deeds under RSA 356-B.

questions.question_text: A buyer purchases a condo unit in NH. What ownership interest does the buyer receive in the common areas?
questions.correct_answer: An undivided interest in common areas
distractors: A leasehold interest in common areas
A tenancy at will in common areas
questions.explanation: Under the NH Condominium Act (RSA 356-B), condo owners hold fee simple title to their individual unit plus an undivided interest in the common areas of the condominium.

questions.question_text: Under RSA 477:44, when does manufactured housing become real estate in NH?
questions.correct_answer: When placed on site and connected to utilities
distractors: When the owner obtains a building permit
When the title is transferred at the DMV
When the home is older than 10 years
questions.explanation: RSA 477:44 provides that manufactured housing becomes real estate when it is placed on the site AND connected to utilities. After conversion, it is conveyed by deed rather than certificate of title.

questions.question_text: A manufactured housing park is being sold. How many days do tenants have to match the buyer's offer?
questions.explanation: Under RSA 205-A:21, when a manufactured housing park is sold, tenants must be notified and given 60 days to exercise their right of first refusal by matching the offer.

questions.question_text: A park owner raises rent without proper notice. How many days advance notice is required under RSA 205-A?
questions.explanation: RSA 205-A requires park owners to give 60 days advance notice for rent increases. The notice must clearly state the new amount and the effective date.

questions.question_text: A manufactured housing park is being sold. What right do current tenants have under NH law?
questions.correct_answer: A right of first refusal to match the buyer's offer
distractors: The right to block the sale entirely
The right to a reduced purchase price
No rights in the park sale process
questions.explanation: Under RSA 205-A:21, when a manufactured housing park is sold, tenants must be notified and given the right of first refusal to match the buyer's offer. This protects tenants from displacement by giving them the opportunity to purchase the park collectively.

questions.question_text: How does RSA 205-A define a manufactured housing park?
questions.correct_answer: Two or more manufactured houses on a site
distractors: Five or more manufactured houses on a site
Ten or more manufactured houses on a site
Any land zoned for manufactured housing
questions.explanation: RSA 205-A defines a manufactured housing park as a property with two or more manufactured houses. This low threshold means most multi-unit manufactured housing sites are subject to the park regulations.
